canonical.definition: The number of moles of a substance dissolved in 1 liter (L) of solution.
examples: {'example': 'Calculate the molarity of a solution obtained by mixing 50 mL of 5 M K₂Cr₂O₇ and 50 mL of 2 M K₂Cr₂O₇ in acidic medium.', 'solution': 'v.f. of K₂Cr₂O₇ = 6\nN_f = (N₁ V₁ + N₂ V₂) / (V₁ + V₂) = ((5 × 6 × 50) + (2 × 6 × 50)) / (50 + 50) = 21 M'}

{'example': 'Calculate the normality of a solution containing 13.4 g of Sodium oxalate in 100 mL.', 'solution': 'N = (wt. in g/eq. wt.) / vol. of solution in litre\nHere, eq. wt. of Na₂C₂O₄ = 67 so N = (13.4/67) / (100/1000) = 2 N'}

canonical.definition: The number of equivalents of a substance dissolved in 1 liter (L) of solution.
examples: {'example': 'Calculate the normality of a solution obtained by mixing 50 mL of 5 M K₂Cr₂O₇ and 50 mL of 2 M K₂Cr₂O₇ in acidic medium.', 'solution': 'N_f = (N₁ V₁ + N₂ V₂) / (V₁ + V₂) = ((5 × 6 × 50) + (2 × 6 × 50)) / (50 + 50) = 21 N'}

{'example': 'Calculate the normality of a solution containing 13.4 g of Sodium oxalate in 100 mL.', 'solution': 'N = (wt. in g/eq. wt.) / vol. of solution in litre\nHere, eq. wt. of Na₂C₂O₄ = 67 so N = (13.4/67) / (100/1000) = 2 N'}

canonical.definition: Measurement of hardness in terms of parts per million (ppm) of CaCO₃ or equivalent to it.
formulae: {'formula': 'Hardness in ppm = (mass of CaCO₃ / Total mass of solution) × 10^6'}

canonical.definition: A method of determining molar masses of solutes using osmotic pressure.
examples: {'example': 'Measurement of osmotic pressure provides another method of determining molar masses of solutes. This method is widely used to determine molar masses of proteins, polymers and other macromolecules.', 'solution': ''}
